mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 60% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery that drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflect supply-side bottlenecks that force immediate equity repricing and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of industrial production contraction months during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 0.3 percent month-over-month forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.3 percent monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate revenue expansion and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months with positive payroll growth or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weakening final demand, which signals impending earnings revisions and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of occurrences or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing inflation during a post-recession recovery phase reflects efficient capacity utilization that boosts profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where industrial production growth exceeds inflation growth during recovery regimes.
